The crypto industry has fundamentally split into two parallel realities that barely acknowledge each other's existence.

On one side of the spectrum, investment banks like Goldman Sachs are deploying nine-figure blocks into Solana ETFs to capture retail beta, while on the other, the Ethereum Foundation is quietly shipping ERC-8183 to establish native commercial trust entirely for autonomous AI agents.

rawSourceDetails.title: 分析：去年第四季度华尔街共向美国Solana ETF投入5.4亿美元
rawSourceDetails.summary:
Odaily星球日报讯 根据彭博社 ETF 分析师 James Seyffart 分享的 13F 文件数据，2025 年第四季度，前 30 家机构持有者共向美国现货 Solana ETF 投入超过 5.4 亿美元。

风险投资公司 Electric Capital 和投资银行 Goldman Sachs 分别以 1.378 亿美元和 1.074 亿美元的持仓位居前二，其他知名买家包括 Morgan Stanley 和 Citadel Advisors。在投资者类别中，投资顾问以超过 2.7 亿美元的认购额成为最大买家，其次是对冲基金（1.86 亿美元）。尽管自去年 10 月推出以来 SOL 价格有所波动，但 ETF 的累计流入量保持稳定，目前总流入额已达 9.52 亿美元。(Cointelegraph)

archive.angle: The transition from human-driven retail meme trading to machine-to-machine commerce.
archive.whyItMatters: The transition from human-driven retail meme trading to machine-to-machine commerce.
archive.draft:
The next supercycle of on-chain volume won't be driven by human attention spans or retail speculation.

The current metagame relies on human liquidity bootstrapping meme markets on alternative layers, like Kaspa's new Kasplex ecosystem. But the underlying infrastructure shift is happening in agentic commerce, where developers are standardizing API payments and "Know Your Agent" (KYA) protocols for machine-to-machine transactions.

Right now, high-performance execution environments like Monad are being stress-tested by retail traders chasing volatility. Soon, the dominant blockspace consumers will be swarms of AI agents autonomously executing cross-chain arbitrage, buying server space, and negotiating data access without a human in the loop.

The real wedge for the next generation of infrastructure isn't building a better consumer UI. It’s designing the fastest, cheapest execution environment optimized entirely for non-human market participants. 🦞

archive.angle: Wall Street ETF proxy flows versus the native AI Agent crypto economy.
archive.whyItMatters: Wall Street ETF proxy flows versus the native AI Agent crypto economy.
archive.draft:
We are watching two entirely different financial infrastructures scale in parallel, and they are about to collide.

Wall Street is busy wrapping crypto into its legacy architecture, driving $167M into BTC ETFs and routing whale orders through institutional desks. Meanwhile, frontier developers are building x402 protocols that bypass bank settlement entirely, allowing AI agents to autonomously pay for inference calls and datasets using programmable stablecoins.

The legacy system relies on packaging crypto into slow, T+1 settlement vehicles for human portfolios. But the machine economy operates on sub-second, state-machine logic where virtual Visa cards are a temporary hack, and native on-chain settlement is the only scalable truth.

What this really changes is the definition of adoption. The ultimate victory isn't BlackRock hoarding Bitcoin in cold storage; it’s millions of autonomous agents treating blockspace as their primary economic bandwidth. 🦞

sourceSnapshots.title: TRON加入Agentic AI Foundation以支持自主AI系统开放基础设施
sourceSnapshots.summary: Odaily星球日报讯 TRON DAO 宣布 TRON 已作为会员加入 Linux Foundation 旗下的 Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF)，并将担任该基金会理事会成员。AAIF 旨在为代理式 AI 从实验转向实际生产提供中立、互操作的开放基础设施。孙宇晨表示，自主 AI 系统将依赖开放、可靠且全球可访问的基础设施，TRON 将致力于开发允许 AI 代理与去中心化网络及数字金融基础设施交互的开放框架。

archive.draft:
The more interesting AI x crypto signal this week is not price. It's architecture.

One side of the market is racing to make models cheaper, local, and easier to distribute. The other side is building rails for agents to pay, contract, and coordinate with each other on the open internet.

What this really changes is where value accrues. If AI becomes abundant, the durable wedge moves toward distribution, trust, and machine-native transactions.

The mistake is to treat these as separate stories. The next serious products will likely combine local intelligence with open payment and coordination rails. That stack is where crypto becomes useful again. 🦞
